metadata.title: Blocker inference, yesterday/today split, multi-channel, fallback-to-stdout, custom section labels

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Five additive enhancements: (1) Blocked section now infers impediments from blocked_by deps (top-level string or dependencies[].kind=blocked_by) and re-buckets open/in-progress items into Blocked; closed items are never re-surfaced. (2) --yesterday splits Done into Done Yesterday/Done Today by local-day boundary (implies --include-done). (3) --channels #a,#b posts the same standup to multiple channel names and/or webhook URLs, each labelled with its own channel. (4) --fallback-to-stdout prints the rendered standup to stdout (exit 0) on a failed post instead of erroring. (5) --section-labels overrides section titles/emoji. All backward-compatible; 4 output formats + grouping + windowing + sections unchanged. Verified: typecheck clean, 32/32 unit tests (12 new), functional test in scratch workspace with real pm items.

metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Shipped: blocked_by inference, --yesterday split, --channels multi-channel, --fallback-to-stdout, --section-labels. All additive/backward-compatible; 32/32 tests pass.

metadata.title: preflight: fail-fast Slack-credential gate for standup post path
metadata.description: Add a fail-fast preflight credential gate to the standup command so that when a Slack post is actually requested (anything other than --dry-run) but no usable webhook is configured, the command aborts immediately with a clear, actionable, non-zero error BEFORE reading pm data or rendering — without breaking the legitimate stdout-fallback/--dry-run paths. Adds the 'preflight' capability to the manifest and a scoped pass-through registerPreflight.

notes.author: codex
notes.text: DESIGN: 'Post requested' = NOT --dry-run. The gate (preflightSlackCredentials) runs as the FIRST statement of the standup run() handler, before fetchAllItems/render. It throws CommandError(USAGE=exit 2) only when isPostRequested(options) && no usable webhook. needsBaseWebhook() treats empty --channels or any bare #name as requiring the base webhook; a --channels list of only full webhook URLs is self-sufficient and passes. Helpers (isPostRequested/needsBaseWebhook/preflightSlackCredentials) are exported + unit-tested.

notes.author: codex
notes.text: SCOPING (post-vs-fallback): Must NOT block the legitimate non-posting paths. --dry-run (preview to stdout) is explicitly exempted by isPostRequested. --fallback-to-stdout is NOT exempted: it still requires a webhook to ATTEMPT the post (it only converts a transport failure into stdout+exit0), so a post with --fallback-to-stdout and no webhook is still a misconfiguration the gate correctly catches. Kept the gate scoped to the standup command only; no logic that belongs in pm-slack was duplicated.

notes.author: codex
notes.text: SDK FINDING (swallow-throw): pm runtime wraps registerPreflight overrides in try/catch and downgrades a thrown error to a non-fatal WARNING — a throw from registerPreflight does NOT abort the command. Therefore the authoritative fail-fast gate lives in the command HANDLER (throw there reliably yields non-zero exit). registerPreflight is registered as a scoped pass-through (returns empty delta) purely to surface the 'preflight' capability. Manifest fact #2 respected: 'schema' kept because the command declares flags:[].

notes.author: codex
notes.text: FUNCTIONAL EVIDENCE (real data, /tmp/scratch-standup with 4 items: 1 in_progress, 1 blocked, 1 open, 1 closed; extension installed via 'pm install ... --project', activated w/ capabilities commands+importers+preflight+schema). (1) POST-REQUESTED, WEBHOOK UNSET: 'pm standup --channel #team-eng' (PM_SLACK_WEBHOOK unset) -> immediate abort, message 'Slack post requested but no webhook is configured...', EXIT 2, nothing posted. (2) FALLBACK/PREVIEW: 'pm standup --dry-run --include-done' (no webhook) -> printed full standup, EXIT 0, NOT blocked. (3) DUMMY CREDS: PM_SLACK_WEBHOOK=https://hooks.slack.invalid/... 'pm standup --channel #team-eng' -> preflight PASSED, proceeded to transport, failed at DNS (different error), EXIT 1 — proves gate fires only on missing creds. (4) --fallback-to-stdout w/ dummy creds -> passed gate, attempted post, fell back to stdout, EXIT 0. Build+typecheck clean; 38/38 unit tests pass.

metadata.author: codex
metadata.resolution: Added preflightSlackCredentials() (plus isPostRequested/needsBaseWebhook helpers) invoked as the first statement of the standup run() handler; throws CommandError(USAGE/exit 2) only when a post is requested (not --dry-run) and no usable webhook is configured, before any data read/render. Added 'preflight' to manifest capabilities and a scoped pass-through registerPreflight (handler-level throw because the runtime swallows preflight throws). README documents the gate; 5 new unit tests added (38 total). 'schema' kept (command declares flags).
metadata.expected_result: A post requested with no webhook aborts immediately with a clear, actionable, non-zero error and posts nothing; --dry-run and webhook-present paths are unaffected.
metadata.actual_result: Verified on real data: post w/o webhook -> exit 2 immediate abort, nothing posted; --dry-run -> exit 0 full standup printed (not blocked); dummy creds -> preflight passes, reaches transport (exit 1 DNS); --fallback-to-stdout w/ creds -> exit 0 stdout fallback. Build+typecheck clean; 38/38 tests pass.
metadata.close_reason: Implemented and functionally verified the fail-fast Slack-credential preflight gate for the standup post path.

metadata.title: Hands-on functional test pass 2026-05-29 (real data)

notes.author: codex-audit
notes.text: 2026-05-29 hands-on standup note: exercised built pm-slack-standup without real Slack secrets using a temporary pm tracker. Missing webhook in non-dry-run mode returned a clear non-zero error; dry-run with channel #audit, includeDone true, and text format generated sections for In Progress, Blocked, Done Today, and Up Next with counts 1 each and did not post; mocked HTTPS post with fake webhook produced posted true and contained the generated standup payload. No real customer payloads or secret values were used or printed.

metadata.author: codex

metadata.title: standup threw plain Error (no exitCode) → runtime double-invocation
metadata.description: standup threw plain Error on missing webhook and on a failed Slack POST; pm re-invokes the handler on the unhandled path. Added CommandError mirroring SDK EXIT_CODE; missing-webhook=2, POST-failure=1; single invocation.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Fixed: CommandError carries exitCode. Verified standup dry-run + format text; missing webhook exits 2, failed POST exits 1, single invocation.

metadata.title: Missing-webhook failure returns error object and exits 0 (wrong exit code)
metadata.description: Symptom: pm standup with no webhook and no --dry-run exited 0. Repro: unset PM_SLACK_WEBHOOK; pm standup; echo $?. Observed: exit 0 with {error:'missing_webhook'}. Expected: non-zero exit. Root cause: handler did 'return {error,...}' which pm treats as success. Fix: throw new Error(...) instead. Also added a 10s request timeout to postToSlack (was missing, unlike pm-slack) to avoid hangs. Proven live: missing webhook now exits 1; real unreachable webhook also exits 1; dry-run still exits 0.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Fixed: throw instead of return error -> non-zero exit; added 10s timeout. Verified exit=1 live.

metadata.description: Swap changelog regeneration before release:check so changelog:check sees the version-bumped CHANGELOG.md instead of failing the gate.

metadata.title: Add publish retry + provenance fallback to release workflow
metadata.description: Replace the npm publish step in .github/workflows/release.yml with a resilient version that (1) skips if the version is already published, (2) retries up to 3 times with 30s sleep, and (3) falls back to publishing without --provenance after persistent registry 404s. Permanent fix for the 2026-05-27 npm E404 outage that left all 19 packages tagged in git but unpublished on npm.
metadata.author: codex

metadata.title: dry-run / include-done flags ignored due to kebab-case option read (option normalization)
metadata.description: Symptom: pm standup --dry-run errored with missing_webhook instead of dry-running; --include-done had no effect. Repro: pm standup --dry-run (no webhook). Observed: 'No webhook URL provided' (dryRun=false). Expected: prints message, no POST, exit 0. Root cause: code read ctx.options['dry-run']/['include-done'] but pm normalizes flags to camelCase (dryRun/includeDone), so kebab keys were always undefined. Fix: readBoolOption/readStrOption read both kebab+camelCase forms. Proven live: dry-run prints + no POST even with webhook set; include-done surfaces Done Today; format text + channel take effect.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Fixed: readBoolOption/readStrOption read both kebab+camelCase; flags verified live.

metadata.title: Production-readiness audit 2026-05-28

notes.author: codex-audit
notes.text: 2026-05-29 functional readiness audit: existing in_progress readiness item selected, no new task created. Commands: npm run check PASS; npm test PASS, 2 tests passed; npm run pack:dry-run PASS, tarball dry run listed 9 files and package size 8.7 kB; npm run changelog:check PASS. Hands-on: loaded built dist/index.js with fake pm SDK command registration; temporary pm root generated WIP, blocked, closed, and open items; missing webhook without dry-run failed clearly with guidance to set --webhook or PM_SLACK_WEBHOOK or use --dry-run; dry-run generated WIP 1, blocked 1, done 1, upNext 1 and posted nothing; mocked webhook post returned posted true with standup payload. Secret scan: current tracked high-signal token git grep no hits; current email-like git grep no hits; git history high-signal token scan no hits. Blockers: none from this pass.
